etymology.origin: Latin magus ← Greek magos ← Old Persian maguš
etymology.note: The magi were the Zoroastrian priestly caste of Persia. Their name is the root of magic, magician, and the biblical ‘wise men.’
etymology.hook: From a Persian priesthood to a fantasy class.

flavor_snippets.html: "What was once formed by masons, shaped by smiths, or given life by <mark>mages</mark>, I will return to the embrace of the earth."
The audience quickly realized a few things: it wasn't a <mark>magic</mark> trick, there wasn't candy in there, and they'd need new clothes.
Call it the thinking <mark>mage</mark>'s version of brute force.
Like thousands of others, the beebles quit <mark>Magic</mark> for several years following the release of the *Mercadian Masques* set.

"For those of courage, even the sky holds no limit."
—Arnjlot Olasson, sky <mark>mage</mark>
"While you were wasting time with abstract equations, I mastered ancient Oggyar fire <mark>magic</mark>. Your move."
On Mirrodin, <mark>mages</mark> who call upon the power of rust are shunned by their people and often turn mercenary to survive.

"Obsession is Zur's one flaw as a <mark>magic</mark> user. His total preoccupation with immortality has become an affliction for his otherwise brilliant mind."
—Jodah, Archmage Eternal
With a steady diet of lawbreakers, Azorius griffins inevitably develop a resistance to the contraband <mark>magic</mark> that taints much of their prey.
"I know what you're thinking," Jace whispered in the <mark>mage</mark>'s mind. "It's not going to work."
Trained with the aid of rhystic <mark>magic</mark>, they become uneasy in its absence.
First discovered in ancient Netheril, chardalyn stones absorb and hold <mark>magical</mark> energy, enabling constructs to be infused with fearsome power and unnatural malice.

mechanics_snippets.html:
When this creature enters, choose one —
• Cure Wounds — You gain 2 life.
• Dispel <mark>Magic</mark> — Destroy target enchantment.
• Gentle Repose — Exile target card from a graveyard.
Exile New <mark>Magic</mark> Game Plus, then restart the game, except leave lands and Equipment on the battlefield and leave this card in exile.

When you encounter this phenomenon, open a <mark>Magic</mark>: The Gathering booster pack and shuffle it without looking at its contents. Those cards become the booster deck. You become the monarch. For the rest of the game, at the beginning of the monarch's end step, that player draws a card from the booster deck. They may spend mana of any type to cast those cards. (Then Planeswalk away from this phenomenon.)
Each creature gets +1/+1 for each different Universe among permanents its controller controls. (<mark>Magic</mark>: The Gathering and D&D each count as a universe.)

When Tricky <mark>Mage</mark> enters the battlefield, if you're on the Mirran team, search your library for an artifact card with mana value 2 or less, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle. If you're on the Phyrexian team, search your library for an artifact card with mana value 6 or greater, reveal it, and put it into your hand, then shuffle.

Pursuant to subsection 3.1(4) of Richard's Rules of Order, at the beginning of the upkeep of each participant in this game of the <mark>Magic</mark>: The Gathering® trading card game (hereafter known as "PLAYER"), that PLAYER performs all actions in the sequence of previously added actions (hereafter known as "ACTION QUEUE"), in the order those actions were added, then adds another action to the end of the ACTION QUEUE. All actions must be simple physical or verbal actions that a player can perform while sitting in a chair, without jeopardizing the health and security of said PLAYER.

Destroy target creature. You may search your library and/or graveyard for a card named Liliana, Death <mark>Mage</mark>, reveal it, and put it into your hand. If you search your library this way, shuffle.

Rule Zero — Whenever this creature enters the battlefield, and before the game begins if this creature is your commander, you may propose a new rule that is contained to this game of <mark>Magic</mark>. If all players in the game agree to it, that rule becomes true for this game. If one or more players decline, instead you scry 2. (If this is your commander, scry after keeping your hand.)
